description_long: This is a streamlined, twin-engine airplane with a smooth, metallic fuselage and a classic mid-20th-century design. The wings are straight and mounted low on the fuselage, each supporting an engine nacelle beneath. The tail features a conventional configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, with multiple windows along the side indicating a passenger or crew compartment. The landing gear is visible and appears to be a tricycle configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a nose wheel. The overall finish is a light gray or silver, giving the airplane a polished, utilitarian appearance.
description: A streamlined, twin-engine airplane with a low-mounted straight wing, conventional tail, and tricycle landing gear, finished in light gray.
description_view_0: Side view showing the twin engines under the wings, cockpit windows, and the extended landing gear.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, engine placement, and the symmetrical tail structure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the fuselage windows, engine nacelles, and the tail's vertical stabilizer.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the nose, cockpit, landing gear, and the straight alignment of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted under each wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a single nose wheel, fixed and extended.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
